methodology.summary: A three-phase new-hire ramp framework based on Michael Watkins's Learn / Contribute / Execute cadence.
Reusable template for agency contractor jd template that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.

A methodology for defining and communicating the unique value employees receive in exchange for their skills and commitment, structured around five pillars: Compensation, Benefits, Career, Work Environment, and Culture/Purpose.
Strategy and execution methodology for building and maintaining an employer brand across Glassdoor, LinkedIn, careers page, employee testimonials, and content calendars.

Bundle of structured-interview methodologies covering competency definition, STAR behavioral questions, scorecards, technical assessments, culture-fit assessment, reference checks, and hiring-committee debrief.

An end-to-end framework for designing and operating a repeatable new-hire onboarding program: preboarding, Day 1 orientation, buddy pairing, 30-60-90 day progression, feedback loops, manager guides, and remote adaptations.
Structured first-30-day onboarding framework covering orientation (Week 1), foundation building (Week 2), and deep-dive with first deliverable (Weeks 3-4).

A two-phase framework for structured new-hire progression after the initial 30-day learning phase: Contribute (days 31-60, first owned project, active collaboration) and Execute (days 61-90, independent delivery, measurable results).
A full-cycle talent acquisition framework covering ten methodologies from multi-channel sourcing through offer management.
A stage-by-stage framework for measuring and improving candidate conversion across six hiring phases: Awareness, Application, Screening, Interview, Offer, and Onboarding.

A paired framework covering two related HR domains: employee retention (stay interviews, exit analysis, 9-box talent reviews, internal mobility) and hiring compliance (legal requirements per funnel stage, DEI practices, process audits).
Worked examples of strong, weak, and red-flag STAR responses paired with probing scripts for each failure mode, plus an interviewer training module and quality assurance checklist.

The STAR framework (Situation-Task-Action-Result) is a structured behavioral interviewing technique that elicits four-part responses from candidates, forcing specificity and comparability across candidates.
The STAR framework (Situation-Task-Action-Result) is a behavioral-interview method for extracting evidence of past performance and structuring post-interview analysis.

A six-step process for building a consistent, predictive interview process: define 4-6 competencies, write standardized behavioral and situational questions, build a scored rubric with behavioral anchors, train interviewers, execute identically for every candidate, and debrief with independent scores before discussion.
